D1_Justification: EA Renewable Energy Production: 5/110 pts (~4.5%); indirect contribution via Optimize Energy Performance up to 18pts
D2_Justification: No distinction between BIPV and BAPV; generic 'on-site renewable energy'
D3_Justification: Only rooftop PV typically counted; facade PV can contribute but no specific pathway
D4_Justification: No mandatory solar study; Integrative Process credit encourages but does not require
D5_Justification: 1-5% energy from renewables for points; no net-zero requirement in base system
D6_Justification: Commissioning required; no ongoing PV performance verification
D7_Justification: PV only counts in Energy category; no crossover to Materials or envelope
D8_Justification: Innovation credit available for exemplary renewable energy performance

D1_Justification: Ene01 up to 15 credits for carbon reduction (~11% of base); Ene04 LZC feasibility 1-2 credits
D2_Justification: No explicit BIPV term; treats all renewables generically under LZC technologies
D3_Justification: Feasibility study considers all renewables; roof PV most common outcome; facade mentioned in guidance but not scored separately
D4_Justification: Ene04 MANDATORY Low/Zero Carbon feasibility study at design stage
D5_Justification: Full Ene01 credits require net-zero carbon; very ambitious
D6_Justification: Post-construction commissioning; operational verification via BREEAM In-Use (separate scheme)
D7_Justification: PV reduces carbon in Energy; minor link to envelope thermal modeling
D8_Justification: Innovation credit for exemplary net-zero carbon performance

D1_Justification: Renewable Energy section: up to 20 bonus points for solar PV (~20% of 100-point base); highest direct PV weight of any system
D2_Justification: BIPV mentioned in guidance documents; solar feasibility study references facade PV; GM Super Low Energy requires renewables
D3_Justification: Roof PV primary pathway; facade BIPV recognized in Super Low Energy programme; shading PV encouraged
D4_Justification: Solar feasibility study mandatory in GM:2021; design-stage integration required
D5_Justification: Super Low Energy requires significant renewable contribution (>60% energy reduction); not full net-zero
D6_Justification: Post-occupancy energy audit mandatory; actual PV output verified against design predictions
D7_Justification: PV primarily in Energy Efficiency section; some crossover with envelope in SLE buildings
D8_Justification: Innovation points for exceeding renewable energy targets or novel BIPV applications

D1_Justification: Energy category ~25 points for GHG reduction on 100-point scale; PV contributes to achieving these via carbon modeling
D2_Justification: No specific BIPV credit; PV treated generically within whole-building carbon modeling
D3_Justification: Roof PV is standard; facade PV can contribute to carbon model but no dedicated pathway
D4_Justification: No mandatory solar study; integrated design encouraged but not prescriptive for PV
D5_Justification: 6-Star (World Leader) effectively requires net-zero carbon; PV essential for top tier
D6_Justification: As-built verification required; operational performance via NABERS (separate scheme)
D7_Justification: PV counted in Energy; minor interaction with Materials for lifecycle assessment
D8_Justification: Innovation Challenge credits can reward novel BIPV integration or net-positive energy

D2_Justification: No BIPV distinction; generic 'renewable energy utilization'
D3_Justification: Roof PV standard; facade solar thermal mentioned; no specific facade BIPV pathway
D5_Justification: Requires ≥2% renewable energy contribution for credits; modest target
D6_Justification: One-year post-occupancy operational data required for verification
D7_Justification: PV only in Energy section; no crossover
D8_Justification: Innovation credit exists but not specifically for renewables

D1_Justification: EU1 Renewable Energy: 5+5 bonus credits out of ~120 (~4-8%); 1 credit for feasibility study
D2_Justification: No BIPV-specific language; 'on-site renewable energy systems'
D3_Justification: Roof PV dominant; limited roof area in high-rises acknowledged; facade PV mentioned in guidance
D4_Justification: Solar feasibility study earns 1 credit (EU1); encouraged but not mandatory prerequisite
D5_Justification: Full base credits at only ~2% energy from renewables; very low ambition
D6_Justification: Commissioning verification; no ongoing operational M&V for PV
D7_Justification: PV only counts in Energy Use section
D8_Justification: Bonus credits (5 extra) for exceeding 2% renewable; generic innovation credit

D1_Justification: Energy category: renewable energy sub-items within LR3 (~3-4% equivalent weight via BEE calculation)
D2_Justification: No BIPV distinction; renewable energy treated generically
D3_Justification: Roof PV standard; limited facade PV discussion in guidance
D4_Justification: No mandatory solar feasibility study requirement
D5_Justification: Level-based scoring; renewable energy is one of many factors; modest contribution expected
D6_Justification: Operational performance data used in CASBEE for Existing Building (separate tool)
D7_Justification: PV within energy load reduction (LR); no crossover to Q factors
D8_Justification: No specific innovation pathway for renewable energy

D1_Justification: ENV1.1 Life Cycle Assessment includes PV; ENV2.1 Primary Energy up to ~10% of total; lifecycle-oriented
D2_Justification: BIPV recognized in facade assessment guidance; lifecycle of PV materials assessed; more explicit than most
D3_Justification: Roof PV standard; facade BIPV explicitly discussed in ENV criteria and building envelope assessment; window PV mentioned
D4_Justification: Energy concept required at design stage; renewable energy study is part of integrated planning requirement
D5_Justification: Requires significant primary energy reduction; nearly-zero-energy building as reference; aligned with EU EPBD
D6_Justification: Lifecycle performance verification; operational monitoring encouraged
D7_Justification: BIPV contributes to ENV (energy), TEC (technical quality - envelope), ECO (lifecycle cost) - genuine multi-category integration
D8_Justification: Innovation/bonus area for exemplary renewable integration
